title: Equals Equals
detail: A hackathon for underrespresented minorities in tech
body: got over 100 students in a room to learn about tech
focused on learning, and wanted to encourage newcomers
saw a problem where many people didn't want to attend a hackathon because they were intimidated
made sure to plan workshops all day so that people who wanted to learn all day could do that
also learned that having workshops attracted more non-men to tech events
not as many black people as expected, but still a good event. People don't know was "underresprested minority means" and thought it meant just all minorities

wanted to focus on marketing to underrperestned students, mostly black and latinx. When I spoke about other hackathons to black and latinx students at my school they had never heard of them. And I realized that they were not being considered in the marketign strategy of these hackathons.
I also realized that almost every group on campus had a hackathon tailed to them (men, women, asian people, jewish people
I taught a workshop on the twitter API and walked a class through creating a basic twitter bot
